query: Can you validate this new checkout modal the way a real first-time user would before we ship it?
We need a go/no-go report on the onboarding flow before tomorrow's release deadline.
Audit this API endpoint and tell me if the error responses actually make sense to a developer.
Test this CLI tool like a real user, not like a linter, and tell me exactly what breaks.
Certify the new dashboard widget is ready to show in front of the customer on Friday.
Harden this support agent's conversation flow so it never fires a broken backend call.
Review this redesigned settings page and make sure we didn't quietly lose any visual quality.
I need an evidence-backed validation pack before we rescue this broken feature in production.
Walk through the signup screen as if you'd never seen it before and flag anything confusing.
Does this pricing page modal actually do what it claims, or is it just decoration that looks nice?
Can you write unit tests for this isolated math utility function that has no UI at all?
Refactor this database migration script so every step is idempotent on rerun.
What shoe size should I order if I normally wear a size 10 men's sneaker in EU sizing?
Write me a product requirements doc for a feature we haven't started building yet.
Fix the failing TypeScript build error that's blocking our CI pipeline right now.
Summarize the latest round of user research interview transcripts into three themes.
Generate a list of SEO keywords we should target for our next blog post.
Optimize this slow SQL query that's running behind the reporting dashboard backend.
Draft a plain-language privacy disclaimer for our terms of service page.
Explain how the OAuth2 authorization code flow works at a conceptual level.
